sourceMovie.title: Ninja Scroll
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Ninja Scroll (1993) as a fantasy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The fantasy and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Demon Slayer -Kimetsu no Yaiba- The Movie: Mugen Train
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ninja Scroll clicked for you, Demon Slayer -Kimetsu no Yaiba- The Movie: Mugen Train (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and animation, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ninja Scroll?
faq.answer: Try Demon Slayer -Kimetsu no Yaiba- The Movie: Mugen Train, Jujutsu Kaisen 0, Road to Ninja: Naruto the Movie—each shares DNA with Ninja Scroll while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ninja Scroll?
faq.answer: Ninja Scroll (1993) is best described as Fantasy, Animation, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ninja Scroll worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
